signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Risk + Carry + Intervention
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.23 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite reports of imminent US/Israeli strikes on Iran. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) remains crowded, creating significant unwind risk if VIX (18.8) breaches 25. Middle East escalation introduces intervention risk as the pair holds near the 160.00 threshold previously defended by the BoJ. Carry dominates the regime until acute risk-off triggers a JPY safe-haven bid.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence + RBA
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7171 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) vs retail 86% short. Institutional vs retail divergence is at an extreme as the market enters the RBA decision. Retail is aggressively fading the institutional long; a hawkish RBA surprise risks a retail capitulation squeeze higher, while a hold confirms the 35bp AUD carry premium. Middle East noise limits immediate upside despite the positioning setup.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Geopolitical Shock
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5875 — COT LF net short -16,833 (23.0% long) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for 27 May. Specs are fading the hawkish CB stance just as drone strikes in the UAE drive a flight to the USD. If RBNZ maintains its hike bias, the crowded spec short faces significant unwind risk. However, the 150bp USD carry advantage and geopolitical stress currently favor the greenback.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Fed Commentary
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1692 — Correlation vs EuroStoxx at +0.725 (z=+1.89σ) indicates a significant break from the -0.142 norm. EUR is tracking equity sentiment rather than its own fundamentals. Fed's Williams boosting inflation views supports the 175bp USD carry advantage, keeping EUR/USD upside structurally limited. Watch for a return to the negative correlation norm to signal a shift back to macro-driven pricing.
